author: gb9t
content: Iteration 53: mfd/mfd-pmic.md committed (46b3422, 236 lines). MFD PMIC family — every smartphone companion PMIC, embedded SBC PMU, laptop power-controller. ~120K LOC across ~250 .c files. PMICs integrate regulators (5-30 rails) + charger + fuel-gauge + RTC + watchdog + ADC + GPIO + sometimes audio/TCPC/fTPM. Grsec: hardware damage primary concern (wrong voltage → silicon burn, wrong charger → battery fire). Defenses: PMIC voltage-setting bounds-checked per regulator (closes CVE-2024-26743 axp20x voltage-set OOB), charger current/voltage limits validated (closes 'malicious userspace sets 100A → battery fire'), watchdog enable+petting CAP_SYS_RAWIO (closes 'unprivileged disables watchdog → silent silicon hang'), ChromeOS EC cmd opcode allowlist (closes 'arbitrary EC cmd → laptop firmware corruption'), EC firmware update CAP_SYS_ADMIN + LOCKDOWN, USB-multifunction dongle descriptor validation strict, power-on sequencing immutable (closes 'malicious reorder → silicon damage'), RTC time-set CAP_SYS_TIME, PMIC-as-fTPM access via SCM only. 53 docs / ~3.09M LOC. Crossed 3M LOC threshold. Next iteration: drivers/leds (already 2 docs) or drivers/input (already 2 docs) or fresh — drivers/cxl/* (already 2 docs); let me pivot to drivers/iio for Industrial I/O (sensors).
